---
title: 'Theological Library'
date: '2022-03-19T21:34:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_type: 'PAGE'
blogger_blog: 'NoHiding.Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_page_id: '540486932673724401'
blogger_filename: '/p/theological-library-your-one-stop-bible.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/NoHiding.Faith/feed.atom'
---



<h3 style="text-align: left;">Your One-Stop Bible School Booklist </h3><h3 style="text-align: left;">You
 need tools for understanding the Bible and biblical authors and your 
favorite celebrity pastor's latest book isn't cutting it.&nbsp;</h3><h3 style="text-align: left;"><br /></h3><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Have you been wishing you could understand the Bible better?</li><li>Have you heard a variety of conflicting opinions that left you confused?</li><li>Have
 you wished you could go to Bible school, even for a few classes, just 
to get a better grip on the Bible for yourself, but you don't have time 
or money?</li><li>Do you have a sneaky suspicion that everything you've 
been taught about the Bible by modern western American Christianity is 
just not quite right (or maybe not right at all)?&nbsp;</li></ul><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><i>Your Theological Library: Build your own personalized theological 
library with hand cultivated suggestions from top scholars. This 
collection has been hand-selected to help you easily step into the world
 of the biblical authors and understand your Bible. - <a href="https://amzn.to/3CTGVYp" target="_blank">Shop Now</a> (affiliate link). <br /></i></li></ul><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container"><tbody><tr><td style="text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/new-test-in-its-world-nt-wright-2b3824c398a0.jpg" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img border="0" data-original-height="499" data-original-width="336" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/new-test-in-its-world-nt-wright-48636e65540c.jpg" width="215" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;"><a href="https://amzn.to/3CTGVYp" target="_blank">BUY NOW: The New Testament in Its World Workbook</a>, by NT Wright</td><td class="tr-caption" style="text-align: center;">&nbsp;(<i>Amazon affiliate link)</i></td></tr></tbody></table><p></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">&nbsp;</h4><h4 style="text-align: left;">You are not alone! Your one-stop-shop Bible School is here below.<br /></h4><p>One of the fundamental issues facing Christianity in the 21st Century is a complete disconnect from the world of the biblical authors. In the modern English speaking west, we are centuries, worlds, and languages away from their world.&nbsp;</p><p>We have a mistaken idea that whatever pops into our head as we read the English translations (or mistranslations) of these ancient texts must be what it "means".&nbsp;</p><p>As biblical scholars Fee and Stuart said: <br /></p><p><i></i></p><blockquote><i>"<b>...a text cannot mean what it never could have meant to its author or his or her readers</b>."</i> <br /></blockquote><blockquote><i>Gordon D. Fee and Douglas K. Stuart, <a href="https://amzn.to/36yGzdq" target="_blank">How to Read the Bible for All Its Worth</a>, 3rd ed. (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan Publishing House, 1993), 74. ((A great resource to add to your library). </i><i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i><i></i></blockquote><p>&nbsp;</p><p></p><h4 style="text-align: left;">But how are we to enter the biblical author's world?<br /></h4><p>There is a great chasm between the world of Biblical Scholarship and the world of the everyday Christian. It has created a dumbing-down of the church.&nbsp;</p><p>After attending hundreds of churches over 30+ years, an astonishingly few pastors quote from the world of biblical scholarship during their Sunday messages (the primary resource for the average Christian). Even fewer are even aware that there is a world of biblical scholarship to engage with.<br /></p><p>If the elections of 2016-2020 taught us anything, it is that Jesus followers are more divided than ever before, and much of it comes from not knowing how to read and understand the written record of how Yahweh works with his people. </p><p>As Dr. NT Wright often says:</p><p></p><blockquote><i>"For too long, we've read scripture with 19th century eyes and 16th-century questions. It's time we get back to reading with 1st-century eyes and 21st-century questions." NT Wright</i> </blockquote><p></p><p>&nbsp;</p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/fb-img-1620060837427-b0dc1c41e595.jpg" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="540" data-original-width="720" height="480" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/fb-img-1620060837427-c3cf6f175750.jpg" width="640" /></a></div><p>&nbsp;</p><p>As Dr. Ben Witherington III says:&nbsp;</p><p></p><blockquote>"A text without a context is a pretext for whatever you want it to say".&nbsp;</blockquote><p></p><br /> As Dr. Michael Heiser says:<br /><br /><blockquote>"You are better off knowing what a passage means than you are memorizing it."&nbsp;</blockquote><p>&nbsp;</p><blockquote>"Second
 Temple literature is really important, and therefore, we ought to pay 
more attention to that material for understanding the Old Testament and 
how the New Testament uses the Old Testament than to our own 
denominational traditions. Now that might sound obvious, pay attention 
to the ancient writers more than you do a modern writer because the 
ancient writers have the worldview of the cognitive frame of reference 
in their head. The later guys donâ€™t. That might sound unbelievably 
simple and elementary and obvious but that is not intuitive for most 
people in church. It just isn't. Theyâ€™re not told about that. They're not
 trained to think that way. They've never seen it modeled for them. The 
Bible becomes this thing that is filtered through their own personal 
Christian tradition and that becomes the meaning of the Bible for them. 
The Bible is used to reinforce something that their group or 
denomination or church believes. And that just isnâ€™t Bible study. Thatâ€™s
 not exegesis."</blockquote><p>&nbsp;</p><h4 style="text-align: left;"></h4><h4 style="text-align: left;">So how to we get the ancient Israelite in our head? And why does it matter?</h4><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="font-weight: normal;"> <i><b>Exegesis </b></i>is a fancy scholar term for the art of knowing what the author intended to communicate to his/her original audience (then and there).</span></li><li><span style="font-weight: normal;"><i><b>Hermeneutics </b></i>is a fancy scholar term for "What does it mean for me?" (here and now). <br /></span></li></ul><p></p><p>Before we can answer anything about "here and now", we must understand the "there and then".&nbsp; <br /></p><p>I invite you to take a step out of the 21st-Century, and time-travel to 1st-Century Palestine to visit a Judean people under occupation by the Roman Empire, and a small band of people following an unusual Rabbi in the northern region of Galilee.&nbsp;</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>What was the situation of this people?</li><li>In what world did they live?</li><li>How did they see this Rabbi and what made them give their lives for him?</li><li>What did they mean when they said...?</li><li>How did a small Jewish Messianic movement turn the empire of Rome on its head? <br /></li></ul><p><b>Why take this journey? </b>Because understanding what God communicated to the original audience has profound implications for the way we live out the Kingdom of God today. <br /></p><p>&nbsp;As Dr. NT (Tom) Wright says:</p><blockquote>"People who believe in the resurrection, in God making a whole new world in which everything will be set right at last, are unstoppably motivated to work for that new world in the present."<br /><br />  "The great drama will end, not with "saved souls" being snatched up into heaven, away from wicked earth and the mortal bodies which have dragged them down into sin, but with the New Jerusalem coming down from heaven to earth, so that the "dwelling of God is with humans"."<br /><br />  "Traditions tell us where we have come from. Scripture itself is a better guide as to where we should be going now."<br /><br />"Jesus's first followers established a new thing - a network of worship-based, ethically rigorous, egalitarian, philanthropic, fictive kinship groups - "church" for short."<br /><br />  "The whole point of the Kingdom of God is Jesus has come to bear witness to the true truth, which is nonviolent. When God wants to take charge of the world, He doesn't send in the tanks. He sends in the poor and the meek."</blockquote><p>&nbsp;</p><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span style="font-size: large;">You need a Theological Library!</span></h3><p style="text-align: left;">This
 page is specially cultivated for the non-scholar. I have put together 
the best books and resources from the best of biblical scholarship. The 
books on this page are aimed at a "lay audience" which means they are (<i>to varying degrees</i>) accessible to most readers, even beginners.&nbsp;</p><p style="text-align: left;">Bible
 College essentially comes down to listening to lectures and reading 
books. The ever-growing <i>(and changing)</i> list of books on this page are the ones I 
suggest you should add to your Theological Library.</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><i>If you can only add one book to your library today, <b>add this one by NT Wright</b>!
 It is your one-stop-shop to the world of the Biblical Authors. The most
 important things you need to know to get out of the 21st-century and 
into the 1st-century Jewish author's mind is between these pages.&nbsp;</i></li><li><i>For other suggestions, keep scrolling.</i><br /></li></ul>

<h4 style="text-align: center;"><b>&nbsp;<a href="https://amzn.to/3CT5tkm" target="_blank">Buy Now</a> - Add this to your library </b><i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i></h4>



  ***
    
   




<div class="card">
  <img alt="Book" src="/images/imported/images-na-ssl-images-amazon-com/417qkgrllvl-sx334-bo1-204-203-200-849d9cb00615.jpg" style="width: 100%;" />
  <h1>A must have for your theological library.</h1>
  <p class="price">Amazon</p>
  <p> The New Testament in Its World: An Introduction to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians, by NT Wright.</p>
  <p><button>   <a href="https://amzn.to/3qj1Zm5">BUY NOW!</a></button></p>
</div>

<span style="color: white;">
  ***</span><br />Amazon Product Information: <a href="https://draft.blogger.com/u/1/#">BUY NOW: The New Testament in Its World Workbook</a>, by NT Wright <i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i><br /><br />Your ticket from the twenty-first century to the era of Jesus and the first Christians. A readable, one-volume introduction placing the entire New Testament and early Christianity in its original context, written by distinguished scholar and author N. T. Wright.<br /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>An ideal guide for students, The New Testament in Its World addresses the many difficult questions faced by those studying early Christianity, including:</li><li>What was the first century understanding of the Kingdom of God?</li><li>What is the meaning of the resurrection in its original context?</li><li>What were the Gospels, and how did they come about?</li><li>Who was Paul and why are his letters so controversial?</li></ul><br />Written for both classroom and personal use, this book brings together decades of ground-breaking research, writing, and teaching into one volume. It presents the New Testament booksâ€”along with their subjects: Jesus and the early churchâ€”within the historical and social context of Second Temple Judaism and Greco-Roman politics and culture.<br /><br />The New Testament in Its World allows you to recover the excitement of what it was like to live as Christians in the first or second centuries.<br /><br />Features include:<br /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Surveys of each New Testament book that discuss their significance and provides commentary on their contents, along with implications for the Christian life.</li><li>Major sections on the historical Jesus, the resurrection of Jesus, and Paul's chronology and theology.</li><li>Up-to-date discussions of textual criticism and the canonization of the New Testament.</li><li>Frequent illustrations, maps, charts, diagrams, and artwork provide additional explanations and insights.</li><li>A distillation of the life work of N. T. Wright on the New Testament with input from Michael Bird.</li><li>Also available are Video and Workbook companion resources (sold separately) to enhance learning and experience the world of the New Testament.</li></ul><div aria-expanded="true" class="a-expander-content a-expander-partial-collapse-content a-expander-content-expanded" style="padding-bottom: 20px; text-align: left;"><span><p><br /></p></span><h4 style="text-align: left;"><span style="font-size: large;">Theological Library Suggestions</span></h4><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="font-size: large;"><b><span style="font-size: x-large;">An ever growing list of theological library must-haves!&nbsp;</span></b> </span><br />
  </p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li><span>Gordon D. Fee and Douglas K. Stuart, <a href="https://amzn.to/3CSmfA5" target="_blank">How to Read the Bible for All Its Worth</a>, 3rd ed. (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan Publishing House, 1993), 74. </span><i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i></li><ul><li><span><span>"a text cannot mean what it never could have meant to its author or his or her readers."&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></span></li></ul><li><span>Gordon D. Fee and Douglas K. Stuart, <a href="https://amzn.to/3KXCY7J" target="_blank">How to Read the Bible Book by Book</a>: A Guided Tour (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan Publishing House, 2014), 74. </span><i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i><span><span>&nbsp; <br /></span></span></li>
      <br />

<div class="card">
  <img alt="Book" src="/images/imported/m-media-amazon-com/41preamrz3l-sy346-78aabdc88651.jpg" style="width: 100%;" />
  <h1>A must have for your theological library.</h1>
  <p class="price">Amazon</p>
  <p> How to Read the Bible for All Its Worth: Fourth Edition, by Gordon D. Fee (Author) &amp;  Douglas Stuart (Author).</p>
  <p><button>
    <a href="https://amzn.to/3N4rbGJ">BUY NOW!</a>
  </button></p>
</div>
  
        <br />

    <div class="card">
  <img alt="Book" src="/images/imported/images-na-ssl-images-amazon-com/41ewx0n1hjl-sx327-bo1-204-203-200-68d2d0a0e89c.jpg" style="width: 100%;" />
  <h1>A must have for your theological library.</h1>
  <p class="price">Amazon</p>
  <p> How to Read the Bible Book by Book: A Guided Tour, by Gordon D. Fee (Author) &amp;  Douglas Stuart (Author).</p>
  <p><button>
    <a href="https://amzn.to/3qjGWjr">BUY NOW!</a>
  </button></p>
</div>
    
    
<br />
  
  <li><span>Ann Spangler and Lois Tverberg, <a href="https://amzn.to/3wi83Py" target="_blank">Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus</a>: How the Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith (Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 2018). </span><i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i></li><ul><li><span><span>"...when he cracked open his Bible 
and read Isaiah 1:2, that he felt the brunt of Tovâ€™s rebuke: â€œI reared 
children and brought them up, but they have rebelled against me!â€ Abegg 
winced, knowing that Tov was using a classic rabbinic technique, quoting
 part of a verse and leaving the rest unsaid."</span>
        <br />

        <br />

<div class="card">
  <img alt="Book" src="/images/imported/images-na-ssl-images-amazon-com/5181ykmxrol-sx326-bo1-204-203-200-db9dff8fb054.jpg" style="width: 100%;" />
  <h1>A must have for your theological library.</h1>
  <p class="price">Amazon</p>
  <p> Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith, by  Ann Spangler (Author) &amp; Lois Tverberg (Author) 
  .</p>
  <p><button>
    <a href="https://amzn.to/36dh2H5">BUY NOW!</a>
  </button></p>
</div>

<br />
    
    
    </span></li></ul><li><span><span> 
    Paauw, Glenn R. <i><a href="https://amzn.to/3wpIcFl" target="_blank">Saving the Bible from Ourselves</a>: Learning to Read &amp; Live the Bible Well</i>. Downers Grove, Illinois: IVP Books, an imprint of InterVarsity Press, 2016. <i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i></span></span></li><ul><span>
  
  <br />

<div class="card">
  <img alt="Book" src="/images/imported/images-na-ssl-images-amazon-com/51giit5pnkl-sx331-bo1-204-203-200-402fa8c2dbf6.jpg" style="width: 100%;" />
  <h1>A must have for your theological library.</h1>
  <p class="price">Amazon</p>
  <p> Saving the Bible from Ourselves: Learning to Read and Live the Bible Well, by  Glenn R. Paauw (Author) 
  .</p>
  <p><button>
    <a href="https://amzn.to/3IpBCRx">BUY NOW!</a>
  </button></p>
</div>
    
<br />
    
    
  
  </span></ul></ul><br /><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>MCDERMOTT, GERALD R. 2017. <a href="https://amzn.to/3wgftmq">ISRAEL MATTERS: WHY CHRISTIANS MUST THINK DIFFERENTLY ABOUT THE PEOPLE AND THE LAND</a>. GRAND RAPIDS, MICHIGAN: BRAZOS PRESS, A DIVISION OF BAKER PUBLISHING GROUP. <span><span><i>(Amazon affiliate link)</i></span></span></li><ul><li>""Rabbis and other Jewish scholars protested for centuries that neither of these propositions was true. â€œHow can you say we believe in salvation by works?â€ they asked. â€œWe say a little Jewish boy at eight days old is brought into the covenant by circumcision. What could he have done to deserve that? And why did God choose Israel? Perhaps the merits of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob had something to do with it, but how do we millions of Jews join the covenant that God made with them? The same way as that little boy, before we have done anything to merit it. It is by what you call grace." 
    
       
    <br />

    <div class="card">
  <img alt="Book" src="/images/imported/images-na-ssl-images-amazon-com/51wb9xmw2kl-sx322-bo1-204-203-200-12b0ffc47ec1.jpg" style="width: 100%;" />
  <h1>A must have for your theological library.</h1>
  <p class="price">Amazon</p>
  <p> Israel Matters: Why Christians Must Think Differently about the People and the Land, by  Gerald R. McDermott (Author).</p>
  <p><button>
    <a href="https://amzn.to/3ijaOrB">BUY NOW!</a>
  </button></p>
</div>
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    <br />
<br />
<br />

    
    
    
    </li><li><p><b>Other Resources</b>: See my <a href="http://www.nohiding.faith/p/resources.html" target="_blank">Resources</a> page for other suggestions, such as Podcasts and Websites, to further your Biblical Studies journey.</p></li></ul>*** <br /><div><br />  <br />*Affiliate: This website includes several links to 
external websites, books, and products. Some, but not all, of these 
links may be "affiliate links". This means we may get paid for anything 
you buy when you click those links. Regardless, I only include links to 
items or places I found personally valuable or I have found helpful in 
solving people's problems and struggles. On that note, if you click on 
any Amazon link it helps this website, if you know you are going to buy 
from Amazon anyway, please click away. <br /><br />  <p>

    
    
    <br />

</p><p style="font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Â©
Topos Creative LLC </span><span style="font-family: Calibri;">March 02, 2022</span></p><div style="text-align: center;"><script type="text/javascript">
amzn_assoc_tracking_id = "toposcreative-20";
amzn_assoc_ad_mode = "manual";
amzn_assoc_ad_type = "smart";
amzn_assoc_marketplace = "amazon";
amzn_assoc_region = "US";
amzn_assoc_design = "enhanced_links";
amzn_assoc_asins = "0310499305";
amzn_assoc_placement = "adunit";
amzn_assoc_linkid = "bf60d56457c469877f7d08a4dd827bf7";
</script>
<script src="//z-na.amazon-adsystem.com/widgets/onejs?MarketPlace=US"></script></div>


<html>
<head>
<style>
.card {
  box-shadow: 0 4px 8px 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2);
  max-width: 250px;
  margin: auto;
  text-align: center;
  font-family: arial;
}

.price {
  color: grey;
  font-size: 22px;
}

.card button {
  border: none;
  outline: 0;
  padding: 12px;
  color: white;
  background-color: #FF9900; /* Amazon Orange */
  text-align: center;
  cursor: pointer;
  width: 100%;
  font-size: 18px;
}

.card button:hover {
  opacity: 0.7;
}
</style>
</head></html></div></ul></div>
